prBody:
## Summary

- **`BarcodeScannerView`** (`FitnessTracker/Features/Nutrition/Views/BarcodeScannerView.swift`) — Full-screen AVFoundation camera view using `UIViewControllerRepresentable`. Detects EAN-13, EAN-8, UPC-A, QR, and Code128 barcodes via `AVCaptureMetadataOutput`. Fires `onBarcodeDetected` once and stops the session to prevent duplicates. Handles camera permission requests and denials with actionable user messages.

- **`BarcodeLookupService`** (`FitnessTracker/Services/BarcodeLookupService.swift`) — Thread-safe `actor` that resolves a scanned barcode string to a `FoodItem` by querying the local `NutritionRepository`. Returns `nil` for unknown barcodes; structured for future remote API (Open Food Facts/USDA) extension.

- **`MealLogEntryView`** integrates both: the Barcode tab opens the scanner as a sheet, calls `BarcodeLookupService.lookup`, and either pre-selects the matching food or shows a labelled error with a suggestion to use the custom-food form.

- **`BarcodeLookupServiceTests`** (`FitnessTrackerTests/BarcodeLookupServiceTests.swift`) — 10 new unit tests covering: successful barcode match, multi-item disambiguation, EAN-8 barcodes, unknown barcodes, empty repository, items without barcodes, repository error propagation, exact-match semantics (no partial matches), and concurrent actor invocations.

- **`README.md`** — Updated Services listing to include `BarcodeLookupService.swift`, expanded Nutrition/ directory tree to show all Sprint 3 views, added all test files to test listing, and updated Sprint 3 status.

## Acceptance Criteria

| Criterion | Status |
|---|---|
| Scanned barcode matched against local food index by barcode field | ✅ BarcodeLookupService.lookup() searches FoodItem.barcode |
| Unknown barcode shows actionable error (offer manual search fallback) | ✅ MealLogEntryView shows error + custom food guidance |
| Camera permission denial handled with explanatory prompt | ✅ BarcodeScannerViewController handles .denied/.restricted |

Closes #24
